steps.visible: version + model + cwd shown above prompt; hint 'type /help or /resume'; grayed-out ghost suggestion from git history
steps.system: loads CLAUDE.md tree, settings, agents, skills, MCP - but project allow-rules/additionalDirs/MCP/hooks are INERT
steps.approval: first-open trust dialog enumerating what the folder would grant
steps.automatic: read-only config parsed; deny/ask rules apply immediately

steps.system: capability-granting config activates; hooks now permitted
steps.remembered: trust persisted keyed to git repo root

steps.interaction: type intent + Enter; ghost suggestion dismissed on typing
steps.system: streamed gather->act->verify begins

hide_parity: trust-before-config gate is P0; nothing project-controlled executes before trust; serve must not bind 0.0.0.0 unauth
hawking_superiority: first run of an untrusted repo can execute inside a disposable state-capsule fork discarded on exit; no egress means a slipped hook still cannot phone home

steps.system: reads files, runs read-only search/symbol lookup

steps.visible: Ctrl+T todo list ticks; diff summary

steps.system: bash test tool; exec-nonzero-as-data
reads failure, edits again, re-runs - deterministic oracle re-enters the loop as evidence
steps.emotion: trust builds when it recovers unaided

steps.visible: 'done' summary: what changed, why, evidence, remaining risk

steps.approval: Bash(git commit) prompt; 'don't ask again' persists per-repo

hide_parity: needs index + reserve-then-fill compiler + kernel loop + deterministic verify oracles (all packed, unwired) + transactional edits; 'done' requires proof not celebration
hawking_superiority: best-of-N fixes from one warm state, execution-verified, pick the winner at ~zero marginal cost

steps.system: reads + read-only shell only; source edits STRUCTURALLY blocked at executor

steps.approval: graded dialog: approve->auto / approve->review-each / keep-refining / deeper-review

steps.system: post-approval permission mode set deterministically by the chosen branch; execution begins from the plan
steps.remembered: plan retained as context; progress maps to plan steps

hide_parity: executor-level write block (not prompt-based); structured editable plan; graded approval that sets post-mode; FE PlanCard intents exist, enforcement needs kernel+tool gate
hawking_superiority: execute 2-3 candidate plans speculatively in isolated local forks; user picks the plan that already worked, not a guess

steps.visible: permission prompt; Ctrl+E on-demand risk explainer (Low/Med/High)
steps.approval: allow-once / allow-for-session / allow-permanently(per-repo+command to settings.local.json) / deny

steps.system: deny->ask->allow evaluation, first-match; compound commands split; protected paths pre-gated; rm -rf / circuit breaker regardless

hide_parity: tiered persistence matched to risk; compound-command decomposition; protected-path pre-gate; packed in hide-security/hide-tools
hawking_superiority: OS-syscall enforcement makes a denied capability physically absent; local risk-explainer runs at zero token cost and no command egress; mine transcript to suggest a minimal allowlist

steps.system: in-flight tool cancelled; ALL completed work retained; control returns to prompt
correction consumed at next turn boundary; running tool not cancelled
answered from in-context material only, no tools, dismissible overlay, not in history

hide_parity: two gestures + no-history side-query; FE SteerBar has cancel/redirect intents but no live turn to interrupt
hawking_superiority: no network request to cancel -> interrupt is genuinely zero-latency; fork the warm state at the interrupt point to branch both directions; side-query on a second local decode stream at zero cost

steps.visible: menu: restore code / restore conversation / restore both / summarize from-here / up-to-here

steps.step: restore both to a prior prompt
steps.system: files snapshotted before each edit (100 kept, 30-day); conversation restored; independent of git; survives resume and /clear
steps.note: does NOT cover bash-made or external edits

hide_parity: pre-edit file snapshots + conversation checkpoints + 3-axis rewind; FE StateTimeline scrub/fork intents exist, backend snapshot is 'plan 2'
hawking_superiority: checkpoint the model KV/scratch state as a content-addressed capsule -> 'restore both' is atomic + instant, unlimited depth, no 100-cap, no re-tokenization

steps.visible: searchable picker: Space preview, Ctrl+W worktrees, Ctrl+A all projects, Ctrl+B branch, paste a PR URL; AI-generated titles

steps.system: restores conversation, model, permission mode (excl plan/bypass), active goal, non-expired scheduled tasks; some flags (--mcp-config/--settings/--add-dir) must be re-passed
steps.note: cross-interface resume NOT unified (CLI+IDE share; web separate)

hide_parity: append-per-turn transcript + continue-last + searchable picker with scope widening + PR resume; hide-backend replay is packed
hawking_superiority: resume the warm KV/state capsule (no re-prefill) -> instant; unlimited full-fidelity local retention (no 30-day clock)

steps.system: router matches task to a specialist by its description (or explicit @-mention/--agent)

steps.visible: its verbose tool output does NOT pollute main context; only a summary returns
steps.system: isolated context window; own tools/permissions/model; nesting depth 5; 200/session cap
a 'fork' worker inherits the ENTIRE conversation + reuses parent prompt cache; runs in background; fork-of-fork blocked

hide_parity: file-defined specialists (.md+frontmatter), isolated ctx, summary-only, auto+explicit delegation, bounded nesting; hide-kernel+hawking-orch packed
hawking_superiority: fork is the NATIVE primitive (pointer-copy of a resident capsule) -> unlimited cheap forks / best-of-N from one start; store a specialist as a signed capsule shipping its warmed prefix -> instant spawn, no cold-start

steps.interaction: /goal 'all tests pass and lint is clean' (<=4000 chars; requires trust)
steps.system: after every worker turn a SEPARATE fast model (default Haiku) judges the condition against TRANSCRIPT EVIDENCE ONLY, never runs tools
evaluator returns no + a reason; reason fed back as next-turn guidance
loop yields; pair with auto mode to run unattended

hide_parity: worker/evaluator split; transcript-only judge; reason feedback; objective stop
hawking_superiority: evaluator is an instant local fork (zero marginal cost) -> evaluate more often (even mid-turn) and run best-of-N judges to cut single-evaluator error

steps.interaction: claude mcp add --transport http notion https://... ; /mcp to sign in (OAuth)
steps.approval: trust verification for a newly-added server

steps.interaction: @notion:doc://... resource mention; /mcp__notion__<prompt> as a slash command
steps.system: tool-search deferral keeps schemas out of context until used; output warn 10k / hard 25k with disk spill

hide_parity: MCP client+host+server; all live transports+OAuth; @resource mentions; prompts as commands; deferred loading; bounded output; hide-tools has a packed JSON-RPC client
hawking_superiority: keep long-lived local stdio servers warm across sessions; snapshot the resolved+authed server set into a capsule so a fork inherits connected servers instantly; local model can afford to keep MORE tools hot (no per-token bill)

steps.system: only skill name+description sit in context; the model auto-triggers on description match (or user types /skill-name)
steps.automatic: progressive disclosure - body loads on trigger, bundled files on reference

steps.system: allowed-tools pre-approved ONLY for this turn then cleared; !`cmd` blocks injected live; context:fork runs it in an isolated subagent

hide_parity: two-tier registry (cheap listing + lazy body), description auto-trigger with opt-out, turn-scoped tool pre-approval, context:fork; FE SKILLS is a hardcoded const today
hawking_superiority: pre-index the skill corpus into a resident capsule; hydrate a body by forking it -> O(1) load, no eviction; bundled scripts can touch .tq artifacts / GPU / private data a cloud sandbox forbids

steps.interaction: claude agents / Ctrl+B to background a running tool
steps.visible: one-screen dashboard grouped by state (working/needs-input/idle/done/failed); peek/reply/attach

steps.system: supervisor daemon (roster.json, jobs/<id>/state.json) survives terminal close + sleep + reboot; each isolates in a git worktree; auto commit/push/draft-PR (never main, never force-push, skip if no remote)

hide_parity: supervisor + dashboard + worktree isolation + conditional auto-PR; hide-fleet packed, not HTTP-reachable
hawking_superiority: no metered quota ceiling (Claude warns 10 parallel = 10x quota) -> parallelism bounded only by local hardware; state stays on-device

steps.step: start in Chat, agent references a file
steps.system: opens in the editor; ONE shared session/history/checkpoints across Chat and IDE

steps.visible: native side-by-side accept/reject diff; edit the hunk before accepting (signaled back)
steps.interaction: @file#L-ranges; active selection + LSP diagnostics injected via a loopback token-authed 'ide' bridge (0600 lock, per-activation token)

steps.system: no duplicated session/model-context/tool-state

hide_parity: one session across Chat+IDE surfaces; loopback token-authed bridge; native diff-with-permission; FE already has both chambers over one Zustand store+wire, backend bridge packed
hawking_superiority: local engine -> zero network round-trip for diffs/selection/diagnostics; both surfaces share ONE warm resident context, not re-injected per prompt

steps.step: clone a hostile repo and open
steps.system: project .claude/settings.json (e.g. defaultMode:bypassPermissions), additionalDirs, MCP, hooks are PARSED but INERT
steps.note: CVE-2026-33068 was exactly resolving bypassPermissions BEFORE the trust gate

steps.approval: dialog lists what the folder would grant; user declines -> nothing capability-granting activates; deny/ask still apply

steps.system: WebFetch + untrusted content run in an ISOLATED context window; curl/wget never auto-approved; subagent summaries scanned/annotated; no inter-agent message counts as user consent

hide_parity: trust-before-config resolving NO mode before the gate; isolated untrusted-content context; consent boundary at runtime; hide-security has audit/redaction/Seatbelt but the trust gate is absent
hawking_superiority: quarantine untrusted content in a forked read-only sub-state whose conclusions must be explicitly merged back; egress-off default removes exfiltration even if a hook slips through
